title: Is San Diego Comic-Con Still Worth Attending?
description:
#SDCC2026 #SanDiegoComicCon #ComicCon #Marvel #sdcc 

I skipped San Diego Comic-Con this year, and it wasn’t an easy decision. Between the badge lottery, Hall H campouts, and the increasing feeling that the biggest fans get the worst experience, I couldn’t justify going. Do you agree, or am I completely off base? Let me know in the comments. If enough people are interested, I’ll make a full breakdown.

title: Wait, I Have to Camp?! The Biggest Problem with San Diego Comic-Con
description:
#HallH #SDCC2026 #ComicCon #SanDiegoComicCon #Marvel

Hall H only fits around 6,000 people. Over 100,000 attend San Diego Comic-Con. That's why fans camp out 15+ hours overnight just for a seat at the Marvel Studios panel. Would you camp out for Hall H? Let me know below.

title: Spider-Noir Lets You Switch Between Authentic Black & White and True-Hue Full Color
description:
#SpiderNoirPrime #PrimeVideo #NicolasCage #SpiderMan #Marvel

One thing I was REALLY curious about with Spider-Noir was how Prime Video would handle the Authentic Black & White and True-Hue Full Color versions.

Turns out, you actually get to choose which version you want to watch at the start, and you can even switch between them during the episode.

That’s honestly such a cool way to handle it because the vibe completely changes depending on which version you pick.

Authentic Black & White feels gritty and classic noir.
True-Hue Full Color feels larger and more cinematic.

So now the real question is…

Which version are you watching first?
Authentic Black & White?
True-Hue Full Color?
Or are you switching back and forth?

title: Spider-Noir: Authentic Black & White or True-Hue Full Color?
description:
#SpiderNoirPrime #PrimeVideo #NicolasCage #SpiderMan #Marvel

Just watched the first two episodes of Spider-Noir.

One in Authentic Black & White.
One in True-Hue Full Color.

And honestly, it feels like two completely different experiences.

Authentic Black & White has that gritty detective noir atmosphere.
True-Hue Full Color gives everything a larger cinematic vibe.

So which one are YOU watching first?

Authentic Black & White?
True-Hue Full Color?
…or BOTH?

Let me know in the comments.

@AmazonMGMStudios @PrimeVideo @SPTV @sonypictures @Sony 
@spiderman

title: FINAL SPACE Is Finally Getting The Ending It Deserves!
description:
#FinalSpace #OlanRogers #animation 

One of my favorite animated series of all time is finally getting an ending.

Final Space was canceled far too soon, but creator Olan Rogers fought to finish the story through a graphic novel, and honestly, seeing this series finally return means a lot to me.

I’ll be attending the LA signing event, and after years of waiting, it’s amazing seeing Final Space get the ending it deserves.

If you grew up loving sci-fi animation, emotional storytelling, and chaotic space adventures, this series is absolutely worth your time.

Have you watched Final Space yet?

title: Resident Evil Finally Feels Scary Again…
description:
#ResidentEvil #ResidentEvilMovie #ZachCregger #sonypictures #AustinAbrams

The new Resident Evil movie trailer honestly caught me off guard. Instead of feeling like another action-heavy adaptation, this feels darker, more grounded, and genuinely unsettling in the best way possible.

Directed by Zach Cregger, the filmmaker behind Weapons, this new Resident Evil reboot looks like a complete tonal reset for the franchise. Austin Abrams also appears to be taking a major role after standing out in Weapons, and that collaboration alone has me extremely curious about where this movie is going.

This trailer gave me the feeling I’ve wanted from Resident Evil for years:
fear, tension, atmosphere, and actual horror.

If this movie sticks the landing, we may finally be getting the Resident Evil adaptation fans have been waiting for.

Are you excited for the new Resident Evil movie?

@sonypictures @Sony @imaxmovies @residentevil

title: Daredevil: Born Again FIXED Marvel’s Biggest Mistake
description:
#DaredevilBornAgain #Daredevil #Marvel #mcu  #JessicaJones

Marvel finally stopped fighting what made the Netflix era of Daredevil great.

Daredevil: Born Again Season 2 feels less like a continuation and more like a full correction. Jessica Jones returns, Luke Cage is back, Wilson Fisk fully becomes Kingpin again, and the entire season feels like Marvel finally embracing what fans loved in the first place.

This season made me want to rewatch the entire Netflix Defenders era all over again, and honestly, that says everything.

Full Daredevil: Born Again Season 2 review coming soon.

@disneyplus @marvel

title: Hazbin Hotel Might ACTUALLY Win An Emmy…
description:
#HazbinHotel #Hellaverse #Animation #PrimeVideo #Emmys2026

Hazbin Hotel is officially part of the Emmy FYC conversation, meaning the hit animated musical series from Prime Video is now being considered during awards season.

What started as an independent YouTube animation by VivziePop has evolved into one of the most talked-about adult animated shows online, thanks to its music, voice acting, animation style, and massive Hellaverse fanbase.

If Hazbin Hotel actually lands an Emmy nomination, it would be a huge moment not just for the series, but for independent animation creators everywhere.

Do you think Hazbin Hotel deserves an Emmy?
And if it won… which song should they perform live at the Emmys?

WARFARE (2025) – MOVIE REVIEW 
Just got out of the theater and… wow. Was Warfare a movie or an all-out emotional battlefield? 💥
Directed by Alex Garland, this high-stakes, boots-on-the-ground war drama takes us straight into Ramadi, Iraq, with a Navy SEAL platoon reliving their most intense mission through memories scarred by chaos, fear, and unbreakable brotherhood.

title: The Amateur - Movie Review | What Happens If Mr. Robot Were a Spy?
description:
#TheAmateur #moviereview #ramimalek  

Just saw The Amateur a surprisingly unpredictable movie! But does that unpredictability work in or against itself? Here’s my initial review and thoughts coming straight out of the theater. 
The Amateur Review – What If Mr. Robot Became a CIA Agent?

The Amateur - “Starring Oscar® winner Rami Malek (“Bohemian Rhapsody”) and Academy Award® nominee Laurence Fishburne (“What’s Love Got to Do with It”). The all-new espionage thriller opens in theaters nationwide April 11, 2025.

Charlie Heller (Malek) is a brilliant, but deeply introverted decoder for the CIA working out of a basement office at headquarters in Langley whose life is turned upside down when his wife is killed in a London terrorist attack. When his supervisors refuse to take action, he takes matters into his own hands, embarking on a dangerous trek across the globe to track down those responsible, his intelligence serving as the ultimate weapon for eluding his pursuers and achieving his revenge. The film also stars Rachel Brosnahan, Caitríona Balfe, Jon Bernthal, Michael Stuhlbarg, Holt McCallany, Julianne Nicholson, Adrian Martinez, Danny Sapani, and Laurence Fishburne. “The Amateur” is directed by James Hawes. The screenplay is by Ken Nolan and Gary Spinelli based on the novel by Robert Littell. The film is produced by Hutch Parker, p.g.a., Dan Wilson, p.g.a., Rami Malek, Joel B. Michaels, with JJ Hook serving as executive producer.”

#TheAmateur #moviereview #ramimalek  

Just saw The Amateur a surprisingly unpredictable movie! But does that unpredictability work in or against itself? Here’s my initial review and thoughts coming straight out of the theater. 
The Amateur Review – What If Mr. Robot Became a CIA Agent?

The Amateur - “Starring Oscar® winner Rami Malek (“Bohemian Rhapsody”) and Academy Award® nominee Laurence Fishburne (“What’s Love Got to Do with It”). The all-new espionage thriller opens in theaters nationwide April 11, 2025.

Charlie Heller (Malek) is a brilliant, but deeply introverted decoder for the CIA working out of a basement office at headquarters in Langley whose life is turned upside down when his wife is killed in a London terrorist attack. When his supervisors refuse to take action, he takes matters into his own hands, embarking on a dangerous trek across the globe to track down those responsible, his intelligence serving as the ultimate weapon for eluding his pursuers and achieving his revenge. The film also stars Rachel Brosnahan, Caitríona Balfe, Jon Bernthal, Michael Stuhlbarg, Holt McCallany, Julianne Nicholson, Adrian Martinez, Danny Sapani, and Laurence Fishburne. “The Amateur” is directed by James Hawes. The screenplay is by Ken Nolan and Gary Spinelli based on the novel by Robert Littell. The film is produced by Hutch Parker, p.g.a., Dan Wilson, p.g.a., Rami Malek, Joel B. Michaels, with JJ Hook serving as executive producer.”

description:
#minecraftmovie #minecraft #moviereview 

A Minecraft Movie - Movie Review

Just saw A Minecraft Movie does it break the curse of bad video game movies, or is it just another blocky mess? Here’s my initial review and thoughts coming straight out of the theater. 

A MineCraft Movie - From Warner Bros. Pictures and Legendary Pictures and starring Jason Momoa and Jack Black, “A Minecraft Movie,” directed by Jared Hess, is the first-ever big screen, live-action adaptation of Minecraft, the best-selling video game of all time.

The film also stars Emma Myers (“Wednesday”), Oscar nominee Danielle Brooks (“The Color Purple”), Sebastian Hansen (“Just Mercy, “Lisey’s Story”), with Jennifer Coolidge.

Welcome to the world of Minecraft, where creativity doesn’t just help you craft, it’s essential to one’s survival!  Four misfits—Garrett “The Garbage Man” Garrison (Momoa), Henry (Hansen), Natalie (Myers) and Dawn (Brooks)—find themselves struggling with ordinary problems when they are suddenly pulled through a mysterious portal into the Overworld: a bizarre, cubic wonderland that thrives on imagination.  To get back home, they’ll have to master this world (and protect it from evil things like Piglins and Zombies, too) while embarking on a magical quest with an unexpected, expert crafter, Steve (Black).  Together, their adventure will challenge all five to be bold and to reconnect with the qualities that make each of them uniquely creative…the very skills they need to thrive back in the real world.

Disney’s Snow White (2025) - Movie Review #shorts
description:
#moviereview #disney #rachelzegler 

Just saw Disney’s Snow White. You might be surprised by what I have to say about this movie. Here’s my initial review and thoughts coming straight out of the theater. 

Snow White - “Disney’s Snow White” is a live-action musical reimagining of the classic 1937 film. The magical music adventure journeys back to the timeless story with beloved characters Bashful, Doc, Dopey, Grumpy, Happy, Sleepy, and Sneezy.

“Disney’s Snow White” is directed by Marc Webb, written by Erin Cressida Wilson, and produced by Marc Platt, p.g.a., and Jared LeBoff, p.g.a., with Callum McDougall serving as executive producer, and features all-new original songs from Benj Pasek and Justin Paul.

description:
#mcu #deadpool #disney 

MCU Revival: Deadpool and Wolverine Prove the Magic Isn't Dead

The MCU was never truly dead or dying, but it certainly faced some challenges. Under Bob Chapek's leadership, Disney made several missteps that hindered the studio's creative output. By removing power from the creative heads and prioritizing quantity over quality, Chapek created a toxic environment that stifled innovation.

Fortunately, the return of Bob Iger has brought about a much-needed course correction. Deadpool and Wolverine's incredible success is a testament to the positive changes underway. While it's not a silver bullet that instantly saves the MCU, it's a significant step in the right direction.

What are your thoughts on the MCU's recent resurgence? Share your opinions in the comments below!

title: Alien: Romulus Movie Review - Is This Movie For You?
description:
#alienromulus  #Alien #review 

TLDR; Alien Romulus wastes time with a needless, slow, boring buildup that culminates in anti-climactic gore and few scares.

So, I saw Alien Romulus and went into it really hoping to like it. In fact, I reacted positively to the trailer months ago, thinking, "Wow, this might be the first Alien movie I actually like." I was wrong. Now, is this the worst Alien movie in my opinion? No. In fact, it's the first one I've been able to finish without getting bored—mind you, because I was in a theater.

This movie has a painfully slow buildup filled with irrelevant plot details that could've been conveyed in an email. Seriously, they could've just displayed an email on screen while the characters were flying to the space station and shaved 25 minutes off the movie.

After this sluggish start, the movie finally begins. I felt like they were trying to introduce us to each character with a unique trait, but let's be honest, these characters were nothing more than cannon fodder (and sadly, there wasn't enough of it) and basically NPCs in a video game that I couldn't care less about. So far, we have pointless characters and a slow buildup. Not a great start. I didn't care about any of the characters except for the lead, Rain - played by Cailee Spaeny and Andy - played by David Jonsson, but hey, at least we had cool kills to look forward to—if you can't tell, I'm being sarcastic.

The kills? Well, the trailers pretty much spoiled them all. From the beginning, I knew who was going to die, how, and when. In fact, I predicted the order and the scenarios perfectly after each character was introduced. So, if you haven't watched the trailer, don't, because the movie genuinely gives away every kill. And there are so few. As for the gory elements, I was hoping for more. I thought it would be like 2013's Evil Dead. Instead, it was lame, unimaginative, and every scene was so quick it could've been a PG-13 movie.

As you can tell, I didn't enjoy this movie. My final con is the unnecessary use of CGI to de-age a character. It took me completely out of the movie. Every time he was on screen, I thought I was watching a comedy. Hollywood, if you don't need to use de-aging, don't. The money spent on de-aging Ian Holm could've been used for more cool kill scenes or anything else. They could've kept the character in the movie but used him as an artificial intelligence on a computer screen. Anything would have been better than that awful CGI that looked like a character from Team America: World Police.

Okay, so I didn't really like the movie, but it did have a couple of pros.

The first pro is lead actress Cailee Spaeny. I've liked her since Civil War, and she doesn't disappoint. Her acting skills are incredible, and I look forward to her future career. The second is Andy, I liked his character when he wasn't annoying. Finally, the practical effects were so good that they made the CGI stand out even more, showing that the movie should've leaned into its strengths and used an animatronic for the de-aged android. You'll see what I mean. Anywho, the practical effects were great, and the Xenomorphs looked incredible.

So yeah, that's my review of Alien Romulus. I'm still not a fan.

title: 🎬🍿 THE MCU IS BACK! Why YouTubers Are Embarrassing Themselves! #mcu #shorts
description:
#marvel #robertdowneyjr #disney 

The MCU is back, and I am here to tell you why there are a lot of YouTubers who are lying to you trying to make clickbait videos where they say that the MCU is dead or it’s not doing well, and the truth is that is absolutely false. The MCU is now sitting at $30 billion across all films. They have had some mishaps, yes, but for 22 or 23 films straight whatever the number is , they had success all the way up to Avengers: Endgame, and No Way Home! But now that they have had a couple of mishaps, which honestly you should blame on Bob Chapek, all of these nerds love to make videos like this where they say that the MCU is dead or that it’s embarrassing itself. 

Where were you each time they were successful? 

Deadpool and Wolverine is absolutely dominating at the box office! The response to Robert Downey Jr’s announced return in Hall H was nothing short of fan excitement, yet you’re going to whine about it and say that the MCU is dead or that the MCU was embarrassing themselves. 

Get over yourself, you’re not that cool. The MCU is doing fine. In fact it’s better than Fine! The MCU is actually propping up Hollywood and making money, getting people back into the theaters. 
We as fans and nerds need to start appreciating what we have with the fact that we get so much great content from Marvel, DC and Hell even Lucas Films! And stop criticizing the creators who are making these great movies for us.
